steps.title: Keep one main verb
steps.content: The sentence already has its main verb: "changed." The blank must describe the codes that later changed, not add another finite verb before the main verb.

steps.title: Reject extra finite verbs
steps.content: A, B, and D add finite verb forms, so the sentence would have two main verbs without a legal boundary. A past participle can modify "codes" in "codes assigned by the committee" while leaving "changed" as the only main verb. The answer is C.
